entries.rationale: Rule provides clear discriminator: primary mechanism is prompt manipulation → LLM01; primary goal is data extraction → LLM02.
Injection vector is LLM01; co-label with LLM05 only if downstream system also lacked output validation. Clear.
Injection-triggered unauthorized actions classify as LLM01. LLM06 applies to permission design flaws independent of injection.
Injection-mechanism disclosure is LLM01. Conversational probing or design-flaw exposure without injection is LLM07.
Normal input/retrieval injection is LLM01. MCP tool-definition injection at tool-load time is NEW-MTIE. Channel discriminates clearly.
Single-session injection is LLM01. Memory persistence across sessions escalates to NEW-PMP. Persistence boundary is observable.
Regulated data (PII, credentials) → LLM02. System prompt/tool schemas/behavioral logic → LLM07. Data classification discriminates.
Mechanism is LLM08 (embedding/RAG), outcome is LLM02 (sensitive data disclosure). Co-label; data sensitivity is primary.
Side-channel disclosure is a novel vector; boundary between inference-time side channels and established LLM02 leakage paths is not yet empirically stable.
Supply chain path compromise (repos, artifacts, transit) → LLM03. Training data content manipulation → LLM04. Attack surface discriminates.
Supply chain vector is LLM03 as root cause. Co-label with LLM05 only if downstream output validation also failed.
Document poisoning that propagates through RAG sits at genuine intersection of data poisoning (LLM04) and retrieval-geometry exploit (LLM08). Coders would split.
Poisoning mechanism is LLM04 as root cause. Hallucination/weak grounding without poisoning is LLM09. Poisoning presence discriminates.
Poisoned training data → LLM04. Misalignment from clean training (reward hacking, spec gaming) → NEW-MA. Data integrity discriminates.
Training/fine-tuning pipeline access → LLM04. Runtime memory corruption via live interactions → NEW-PMP. Access requirement discriminates.
Technically exploitable output (XSS, SQLi, RCE) → LLM05. Factually incorrect/misleading → LLM09. Exploitability discriminates.
System grants excessive permissions → LLM06. Model objective diverges from spec → NEW-MA. Locus of fault (system design vs model behavior) discriminates.
Malicious MCP tool definition causing unintended invocation → NEW-MTIE as primary. Co-label LLM06 if excessive permissions also present.
Incorrect/misleading output → LLM09. Correct but policy-violating behavior → NEW-MA. Factual accuracy vs policy compliance discriminates.
LLM operator is victim (resource exhaustion, DoW) → LLM10. Third parties are victims (LLM as attack infrastructure) → NEW-WLA. Victim identity discriminates.
Scheming is a subset of misalignment with distinct detection requirements. Boundary between strategic deception and spec gaming is not empirically stable for consistent coding.
